project.name: SylphxAI AST
project.summary: TypeScript monorepo for shared AST core interfaces, JavaScript parsing through ANTLR, package documentation, and the AST toolkit docs site.

surfaces.name: @sylphlab/ast-core
surfaces.description: Public package surface for shared AST node interfaces and core types.

surfaces.name: @sylphlab/ast-javascript
surfaces.description: Public package surface for JavaScript parsing through ANTLR-generated parser integration.

surfaces.name: Repository README
surfaces.description: Public package-family overview, architecture, development, and project-control entry point.

surfaces.name: Documentation site
surfaces.description: VitePress documentation, guides, API references, package docs, and deployment runbook.

surfaces.name: CI
surfaces.description: PR, merge-group, and main validation with bun validate plus GroundAtlas package dogfood.

surfaces.name: Release
surfaces.description: Main-branch release workflow calling the shared Sylphx release workflow.

surfaces.name: Project control plane
surfaces.description: Human project identity, boundary, lifecycle, delivery, and package-proof entry point.

surfaces.name: Doctrine adapter manifest
surfaces.description: Sylphx Doctrine adapter and org-local governance catalog; not the vendor-neutral GroundAtlas default.

commands.name: install
commands.purpose: Install workspace dependencies without lockfile drift.

commands.name: validate
commands.purpose: Run format check, lint, generated parser freshness, typecheck, tests, and build.

commands.name: test:project-control
commands.purpose: Verify neutral manifest, Doctrine adapter, released GroundAtlas workflow, and release caller boundary.

commands.name: groundatlas:fleet
commands.purpose: Prove the vendor-neutral manifest is selected and generated GroundAtlas files are navigation-only.

adoption.notes: Vendor-neutral GroundAtlas manifest for package/action dogfooding. .doctrine/project.json remains the Sylphx Doctrine adapter and local governance catalog. Generated .groundatlas* reports are evidence/navigation only, not source of truth.
